name: Common pea (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (green garden pea, Pisum sativum, raw)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant free sugar in green pea; individual-sugar panel

components.name: starch
components.source: Derived from USDA FDC 170419 (total carb 14.5 - sugars 5.7 - fiber 5.7)
components.evidence: Fresh green pea; note dry pea seed is 39-46% starch (Rungruangmaitree review, PMC10341148)

components.name: raffinose
components.evidence: Characteristic pea raffinose-family oligosaccharide; present but unquantified in USDA fresh panel

components.name: stachyose
components.evidence: Characteristic pea RFO; present, unquantified for fresh

components.name: verbascose
components.evidence: Characteristic pea RFO; present, unquantified for fresh

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Madurapperumage et al. 2025, Legume Science, doi:10.1002/leg3.70061
components.evidence: Most abundant organic acid in dry pea (27.58 +/- 2.60 mg/100g); dry-pea basis

components.name: tartaric acid
components.evidence: Lowest genotype mean organic acid in dry pea (5.47 +/- 0.81 mg/100g); dry-pea basis

components.name: malic acid + succinic acid
components.evidence: Reported only as a co-eluting group (malic+succinic); both present but not individually quantified

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Protein amino-acid profile; most abundant AA

components.name: vitamin A
components.evidence: Provitamin-A activity from carotenoids (not preformed retinol)

components.name: myo-inositol
components.evidence: Present in pea (with its methyl-ether D-pinitol); unquantified

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: non-nutritive toxic metalloid contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal) vitamin A; plant pea supplies provitamin-A carotenoids only, not retinol -- FooDB implausible/predicted

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: vitamin B12 is absent from plant foods; not synthesized by peas -- FooDB implausible/predicted

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: industrial microbial-fermentation food preservative, not a native pea constituent -- FooDB implausible

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae (celery/parsnip), implausible in Pisum -- FooDB predicted

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

missing_added: individual sugars with amounts (sucrose 4.99, fructose 0.39, maltose 0.17, glucose 0.12 g/100g)
organic acids with amounts (citric 27.58, tartaric 5.47 mg/100g; malic+succinic present as group)
full quantitative free/protein amino-acid profile (18 AAs)
fatty-acid profile with amounts (linoleic, palmitic, ALA, oleic, stearic)
carotenoids with amounts (lutein+zeaxanthin 2476, beta-carotene 449, alpha-carotene 21 ug/100g)
complete quantitative mineral and vitamin panel
keep_uncovered: verbascose (genuine pea RFO -- kept as component)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170419 (Peas, green, raw) -- full proximate/sugar/amino-acid/fatty-acid/mineral/vitamin/carotenoid panel
Madurapperumage et al. 2025, Legume Science, doi:10.1002/leg3.70061 (organic acids in dry pea: citric, tartaric, malic+succinic group)
Rungruangmaitree & Jiraungkoorskul review, PMC10341148 (pea starch 39-46% dry seed)
FooDB FOOD00141 (compound presence for oligosaccharides, cyclitols, phytochemicals)
notes: Curated on fresh green garden pea (USDA 170419). Dominant carbon/energy sources are sucrose (~5 g/100g) plus starch (~3 g/100g fresh; much higher in mature dry seed) with the raffinose-family oligosaccharides (raffinose/stachyose/verbascose) as characteristic but unquantified pea sugars; free glucose/fructose are minor. Organic acids (citric dominant, plus tartaric and a malic+succinic pool) added from dry-pea literature (dry-weight basis) as they were absent from the USDA panel. Protein-derived amino acids (Glu/Asp/Arg-rich) are a major N source. Removed heavy-metal contaminants and biologically implausible FooDB entries (retinol, B12, epsilon-polylysine, oxypeucedanin).
